T0023.title: Fake Follower Boosting
T0023.definition: The black-market practice of inflating a social media account’s follower count through bulk registration or purchase of fake accounts.
T0023.description: Fraud rings leverage bot-farm control systems and SMS verification platforms to mass-produce zombie accounts that are then used to follow a designated target, creating an illusion of popularity. These fake followers typically generate no genuine engagement and are used to facilitate scam-driven traffic, exaggerate advertising performance, or defraud platform creator incentive programs, ultimately misleading real users or advertisers.
T0023.usageExample: A new account going live gets no traffic—first find a channel to pad the follower count and make the storefront look credible, otherwise no one will trust you.
